jokes.text: Goes great with your lipstick.
jokes.setup: Kevin is wearing bronzer because he feels pasty from not getting outside
jokes.punchline: Kevin sarcastically implies the other person is also wearing makeup
jokes.explanation: Solid comeback with good economy, establishes the show's dry wit early

dialogue.line: Goes great with your lipstick.

jokes.text: Now, what is this, some kind of face caulk?
jokes.setup: Kevin examining makeup products in the makeup room
jokes.punchline: Comparing makeup to construction material
jokes.explanation: Clever visual observation with good wordplay, very quotable

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Now, what is this, some kind of face caulk?

jokes.text: Could I have your job? / No.
jokes.setup: Kevin asks casually if he can have someone's job
jokes.punchline: Immediate, flat rejection
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and economy, captures show business desperation

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Could I have your job?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: No.

jokes.text: Hey, mind if I borrow some of your bronzer for my penis? It doesn't get out much.
jokes.setup: Callback to earlier bronzer conversation about not getting outside
jokes.punchline: Applying the same logic to an absurd body part
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback with escalation to absurdist territory, very quotable

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Hey, mind if I borrow some of your bronzer for my penis? It doesn't get out much.

jokes.text: I can't eat anything out of a man's pocket.
jokes.setup: Offered a pita from someone's pocket
jokes.punchline: Overly specific and neurotic refusal
jokes.explanation: Great character establishment - neurotic female writer with specific boundaries

dialogue.speaker: Wendy
dialogue.line: I can't eat anything out of a man's pocket.

jokes.text: Larry does all the punch lines around here.
jokes.setup: Wendy delivers a punchline during her pitch
jokes.punchline: Phil immediately shuts down her comedy contribution
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about comedy hierarchy, perfectly captures writer room dynamics

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: Larry does all the punch lines around here.

jokes.text: As do I. / Something else we have in common.
jokes.setup: Wendy says she wants to break into television
jokes.punchline: Phil sarcastically suggests they have this in common while clearly being established
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic put-down that reveals character insecurity

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: As do I.

dialogue.speaker: Wendy
dialogue.line: Something else we have in common.

jokes.text: You guys sound like an Irish spring commercial.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You guys sound like an Irish spring commercial.

jokes.text: Oh, good welding school. / A lot of arrogant welders there.

jokes.explanation: Artie's classic blue-collar wit, perfectly timed takedown of Phil's pretension

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Oh, good welding school. A lot of arrogant welders there.

jokes.text: Larry, girls just aren't that funny.
jokes.setup: Discussion about hiring Wendy
jokes.punchline: Phil's blatantly sexist statement delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures 90s workplace sexism, gets laughs through uncomfortable truth

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: Larry, girls just aren't that funny.

jokes.text: Yeah, when you quit hookers you put on 10 pounds. That's why I wear the patch. The pussy patch.

jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay and absurd concept, very quotable

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Yeah, when you quit hookers you put on 10 pounds. That's why I wear the patch. The pussy patch.

jokes.text: Dr. Jack kevorkian recently attended his 41St suicide. He announced that the 50th suicide would receive a free tote bag.
jokes.setup: Reference to assisted suicide doctor
jokes.punchline: Treating suicides like a customer loyalty program
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with perfect structure. The 'tote bag' detail is brilliantly mundane against the dark subject

dialogue.line: Dr. Jack kevorkian recently attended his 41St suicide. He announced that the 50th suicide would receive a free tote bag.

jokes.text: Arr, fuck my eye, matey.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Arr, fuck my eye, matey.

jokes.text: Larry doesn't do bunny jokes, OK?
jokes.setup: Writer pitches Energizer bunny joke

jokes.explanation: Good meta joke about arbitrary comedy restrictions, shows Phil's control issues

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: Larry doesn't do bunny jokes, OK?

jokes.text: Well, then you can use it tonight to impress the girls at the whorehouse.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: Well, then you can use it tonight to impress the girls at the whorehouse.

jokes.text: Hey, man, I don't need jokes to impress the girls at the whorehouse.

dialogue.line: Hey, man, I don't need jokes to impress the girls at the whorehouse.

jokes.text: That's great. I love John Wayne. / I thought it was George Gobel. / No, it's Prince Charles. / And you guys can go fuck yourselves.

dialogue.line: That's great. I love John Wayne.
I thought it was George Gobel.
No, it's Prince Charles.
And you guys can go fuck yourselves.

dialogue.speaker: Wendy
dialogue.line: I've written 100 jokes. He hasn't seen one joke?

jokes.text: Fucker.

dialogue.speaker: Wendy
dialogue.line: Fucker.

jokes.text: Your mother must have really fucked you up, Phil.

jokes.setup: Phil dismissing Wendy's jokes as shopping observations
